name: SoonerStart Early Intervention Program
description: Oklahoma's Part C early intervention program for families with infants and toddlers (birth to 3) with developmental delays or disabilities. Provides case management, evaluation, AT devices and services, and builds upon family resources to enhance development through everyday learning opportunities at no cost.

name: The Arc of Oklahoma
description: Promotes and protects the human rights of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, serving all 77 Oklahoma counties. Services include special education advocacy, residential advocacy, caregiver trainings, resource navigation, and bilingual support groups.
address: 2516 E 71st St Ste A, Tulsa, OK 74136

name: Special Olympics Oklahoma
description: Benefits 12,400 participants and conducts more than 175 sports competitions and training clinics every year. Programs include Young Athletes, Unified Sports, and Athlete Leadership University for people with intellectual disabilities.

name: Oklahoma Disability Law Center
description: Oklahoma's federally mandated Protection and Advocacy system since 1977. Provides free legal assistance, facility monitoring, workshops on legal rights, and advocacy to protect, promote, and expand the rights of people with disabilities. Offices in Oklahoma City and Tulsa.
address: 4334 NW Expressway, Suite 124, Oklahoma City, OK 73116

name: Oklahoma Department of Rehabilitation Services
description: Helps Oklahomans with disabilities bridge barriers to employment, school, and independent living. Vocational Rehabilitation division provides counseling, guidance, and job placement. Five program divisions include VR, Visual Services, and Disability Determination.

name: NAMI Oklahoma
description: Oklahoma affiliate of NAMI providing advocacy, free mental health support, online groups, and education for individuals and families affected by mental illness. Local affiliates include NAMI Greater OKC and NAMI Tulsa.

name: Oklahoma ABLE Tech
description: Statewide AT program providing assistive technology devices and services, financing opportunities, digital accessibility services, advocacy, and education. Device loan inventory of over 2,300 devices across 10 categories. Offices in Stillwater and Oklahoma City.

name: Oklahoma STABLE (ABLE Account)
description: Oklahoma's ABLE savings program allowing people with disabilities to save and invest without losing Medicaid or SSI eligibility. Oklahoma residents pay $0 maintenance fee. $25 grant for new accounts. Five investment options available.

name: Oklahoma Children's Hospital OU Health - Developmental Pediatrics
description: Expert developmental and behavioral pediatric physicians and child psychologists at the Child Study Center providing diagnoses and individualized treatment plans. Research focus on fetal drug and alcohol exposure, ADHD, and autism spectrum disorders.

name: Oklahoma Adaptive Sports Association (OKASA)
description: Develops, sponsors, and promotes recreational and competitive athletic opportunities for individuals with physical challenges. Programs include wheelchair basketball, swimming, track and field, and water sports at no charge to athletes. Equipment available to borrow.
